steps.title: Choose the decay base
steps.content: A $23\%$ decrease every period leaves $100\%-23\%=77\%$ of the previous mass, so the multiplier per period is $0.77$ — not $0.23$.

steps.title: Set the period exponent
steps.content:
Each $11.69$ minutes is one period, so the number of periods elapsed is $\frac{t}{11.69}$. The model is $M=100(0.77)^{t/11.69}$.

The Desmos row below graphs the same decay model using $x$ for time and $y$ for mass, so the curve starts at $100$ and multiplies by $0.77$ each period. That matches choice A — A is correct.
